title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument includes RBAC

input_row.title: Ensure that the --authorization-mode argument includes RBAC
input_row.description: Turn on Role Based Access Control.
input_row.rationale: Role Based Access Control (RBAC) allows fine-grained control over the operations that different entities can perform on different objects in the cluster. It is recommended to use the RBAC authorization mode.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --authorization-mode argument exists and is set to a value to include RBAC .
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --authorization-mode parameter to a value that includes RBAC , for example: --authorization-mode=Node,RBAC
input_row.impact: When RBAC is enabled you will need to ensure that appropriate RBAC settings (including Roles, RoleBindings and ClusterRoleBindings) are configured to allow appropriate access.
input_row.default_value: By default, RBAC authorization is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control specifies checking the `--authorization-mode` argument for the kube-apiserver, which is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- The audit mentions running a command on the Control Plane node, but this can be automated by checking the kube-apiserver configuration via the Kubernetes API.
- The configuration of the kube-apiserver can be queried using `kubectl` to ensure that RBAC is included in the `--authorization-mode` argument.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_authorization_mode_includes_rbac

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API or `kubectl` to check the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The command to retrieve the kube-apiserver configuration is:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
  ```
- This command will return the command-line arguments used to start the kube-apiserver.
- The specific property to validate is that the `--authorization-mode` argument includes `RBAC`.
- **Pass/Fail criteria:**
  - **Pass:** The output includes `--authorization-mode=...` with `RBAC` as one of the values.
  - **Fail:** The output does not include `RBAC` in the `--authorization-mode` argument or the argument is missing entirely.
